name: Project launch
goal: Align everyone, agree what you're building, and deliver something real people can react to.
description: A simple three-week shape you can follow: kick off with the right people, write down scope so arguments stop, then ship a first slice. Calendar dates line up with the day you start this plan.

tasks.title: Kick off with stakeholders
tasks.content:
Get the right people in the (virtual) room and make the why obvious.

What to cover:
- Why this project exists and what "good" looks like
- Who decides what, and how you'll communicate
- Known risks, assumptions, and open questions

Before the meeting:
- Send a short agenda 24-48 hours ahead
- Pick someone to take notes and share them the same day

tasks.title: Define scope and write it down
tasks.content:
Turn fuzzy ideas into a shared picture of what's in, what's out, and how you'll know you're done.

Aim for one page you can point at:
- Problem you're solving and the outcome you want
- Clear boundaries (nice-to-haves vs must-haves)
- Dependencies on other teams, tools, or vendors

Close the loop:
- Get a clear yes from your sponsor or product owner so you're not re-litigating scope every week

tasks.title: Ship the first real milestone
tasks.content:
Deliver something tangible: a prototype, a doc set people can comment on, or a thin slice of the real product.

Before you build:
- Write down what "done" means for this milestone

When it's ready:
- Walk stakeholders through it and capture feedback
- Turn feedback into a short list of next steps
- If scope moved, refresh dates so the plan still feels honest
